claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're enquiring about, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I'm enquiring about one child - my son Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

Now, could you please tell me if you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Do you live with Alex, or if not, do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit (currently £25.60 per week for an eldest/only child) towards looking after him - for example on food, clothes, or pocket money?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me, but I do contribute towards his upkeep. I help out with things like clothes, school supplies, and other bits he needs. I give about £26 a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex lives with someone else, I have a couple more questions.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to check eligibility:

Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation provided by a local council, or does he live in a private arrangement with a friend or relative?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in hospital or care or anything like that. He just lives with his other parent in a private arrangement.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

One final question: Is your local council paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards his accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**. Here's a summary of why:

**For Alex (age 8):**
- ✅ Alex is under 16, meeting the age requirement
- ✅ You live in the UK
- ✅ Although Alex doesn't live with you, you contribute approximately £26 per week towards his upkeep, which meets or exceeds the Child Benefit rate (£25.60 per week for an eldest/only child)
- ✅ No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- ✅ The local council is not paying towards his accommodation or maintenance

Because you're contributing at least as much as the Child Benefit amount towards Alex's upkeep (through clothes, school supplies, etc.), you're considered "responsible" for him under the eligibility rules.

**Important notes:**
- You should ensure your contributions continue to be at least equal to the Child Benefit rate to maintain eligibility
- If Alex's other parent were to also claim Child Benefit for him in the future, only one of you could receive it. You would need to agree between yourselves who should claim, or HMRC would decide

You can proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit if you wish. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. Although Alex does not live with the claimant (he lives with his other parent in a private arrangement), the claimant contributes approximately £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep (clothes, school supplies, etc.), which is at least equal to the Child Benefit rate (£25.60 per week for an eldest/only child). This means the claimant is considered 'responsible' for Alex under the eligibility rules. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and the local council is not paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. Therefore, the claimant meets all the criteria to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. They live in the UK and, although Alex lives with his other parent, the claimant contributes at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount towards Alex's upkeep. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and the local council is not involved in his care or maintenance.

This means the claimant is considered 'responsible' for Alex under the eligibility rules. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and the local council is not paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. Therefore, the claimant meets all the criteria to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

They live in the UK and, although Alex lives with his other parent, the claimant contributes at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount towards Alex's upkeep. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and the local council is not involved in his care or maintenance.
